input.question: Count how many customers are in New York

output: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM customers WHERE city = 'New York'
SELECT DISTINCT c.name FROM customers c JOIN billing b ON c.customer_id = b.customer_id WHERE b.status = 'unpaid'

input.question: List the top 5 customers by data usage

output: SELECT c.name, SUM(nu.data_gb) AS total_data FROM customers c JOIN network_usage nu ON c.customer_id = nu.customer_id GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.name ORDER BY total_data DESC LIMIT 5

input.question: Get average monthly rate per plan

output: SELECT plan_name, AVG(monthly_rate) AS avg_rate FROM subscriptions GROUP BY plan_name
SELECT city, COUNT(*) AS customer_count FROM customers GROUP BY city ORDER BY customer_count DESC
SELECT * FROM billing WHERE status = 'unpaid' AND due_date < CURRENT_DATE

input.question: Show total call minutes by customer

output: SELECT c.name, SUM(nu.call_minutes) AS total_minutes FROM customers c JOIN network_usage nu ON c.customer_id = nu.customer_id GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.name ORDER BY total_minutes DESC

input.question: Find customers with no network usage

output: SELECT name FROM customers WHERE customer_id NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT customer_id FROM network_usage)

input.question: List customers on the enterprise plan

output: SELECT c.name, c.city FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id WHERE s.plan_name = 'enterprise'

input.question: Get the highest monthly billing amount
Show customers who have more than one subscription

output: SELECT customer_id, COUNT(*) AS sub_count FROM subscriptions GROUP BY customer_id HAVING COUNT(*) > 1

input.question: Find the average data usage per customer

output: SELECT c.name, AVG(nu.data_gb) AS avg_data FROM customers c JOIN network_usage nu ON c.customer_id = nu.customer_id GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.name
